claim_boundary: These are proof-carrying position-contract reports for declared integer-period phase banks plus numerical real-phase margin scans. They are not model-quality, context-length, speed, memory, perplexity, or deployment claims.

certificate.claim_boundary: Exact pass/fail is for a declared positive integer-period phase bank. No real-valued RoPE, model-quality, context-length, perplexity, speed, memory, training-stability, or deployment claim is made.

rational_margin_certificate.claim_boundary: This is a theorem-backed rational/discretized turn-ratio certificate. It is not a proof of the standard irrational real RoPE schedule unless that schedule is explicitly replaced by the declared rational ratio.
rational_margin_certificate.explanation: Lean proves this reduced rational turn ratio has finite-context nearest-integer margin 1/denominator because the inspected context does not reach the denominator return gap.
rational_margin_certificate.name: rational_turn_ratio_1_4099_context_4096

standard_interval_candidate_plans.claim_boundary: Interval-plan data only unless theorem_status names a compiled Lean seed. Even the proved seed is not a model-quality claim, large-context bank claim, speed claim, or deployment claim.
standard_interval_candidate_plans.explanation: This exact-rational plan groups positive gaps into integer-cell bands for a Lean interval certificate. The d4 margin-1/512 context-333 plan and d6 margin-1/1024 context-710 plan have now been converted into compiled Lean declarations; other generated plans remain source data until matching declarations compile and manifest ids are marked proved.
standard_interval_candidate_plans.name: standard_rope_channel0_interval_plan_d4_margin_1_512_context_333

standard_interval_candidate_plans.claim_boundary: Interval-plan data only unless theorem_status names a compiled Lean seed. Even the proved seed is not a model-quality claim, large-context bank claim, speed claim, or deployment claim.
standard_interval_candidate_plans.explanation: This exact-rational plan groups positive gaps into integer-cell bands for a Lean interval certificate. The d4 margin-1/512 context-333 plan and d6 margin-1/1024 context-710 plan have now been converted into compiled Lean declarations; other generated plans remain source data until matching declarations compile and manifest ids are marked proved.
standard_interval_candidate_plans.name: standard_rope_channel0_interval_plan_d6_margin_1_1024_context_710

standard_interval_certificate.claim_boundary: This is a theorem-backed interval certificate for the genuine standard RoPE channel-0 turn ratio over context 710 only. It is not a full standard RoPE bank certificate and does not certify 512, 4096, or larger contexts.
standard_interval_certificate.explanation: Lean proves that standard RoPE channel 0, with turn ratio 1/(2*pi), has finite nearest-integer margin 1/1024 for gaps 1 through 709. Every gap uses the six-decimal enclosure 500000*gap/3141593 <= gap/(2*pi) <= 500000*gap/3141592, split across computed integer cells 0 through 112. Lean also proves that gap 710 is already within the same 1/1024 margin of integer turn 113, so this exact margin cannot extend to a larger context for channel 0.
standard_interval_certificate.name: standard_rope_channel0_interval_context_710
